result:
## Method

Grepped `src/` for `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE` / `#endif /* CONFIG_SAE */` (also checked `defined(CONFIG_SAE)` and `#ifndef CONFIG_SAE` — none found beyond the plain form). Found **15 `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE` blocks across 10 files** (all within the single compile config covered by `compile_commands.json`). Used `LSP documentSymbol` on `src/ap/ieee802_11.c` to get exact function boundaries, then `Read` to confirm each block's enclosing scope (function body vs. struct vs. header declaration).

## Functions whose ENTIRE body only exists under CONFIG_SAE ("whole")

All 14 are in `src/ap/ieee802_11.c`, inside one contiguous `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE` … `#endif` span (lines 318–876):

1. `auth_build_sae_commit` — line 323
2. `auth_build_sae_confirm` — line 352
3. `auth_sae_send_commit` — line 367
4. `auth_sae_send_confirm` — line 387
5. `use_sae_anti_clogging` — line 407
6. `check_sae_token` — line 430
7. `auth_build_token_req` — line 446
8. `sae_check_big_sync` — line 478
9. `auth_sae_retransmit_timer` — line 489
10. `sae_clear_retransmit_timer` — line 522 (see note below)
11. `sae_set_retransmit_timer` — line 528
12. `sae_sm_step` — line 540
13. `handle_auth_sae` — line 697
14. `auth_sae_init_committed` — line 855

**Note on #10:** `src/ap/ieee802_11.h:97-105` declares this same function as `extern` when `CONFIG_SAE` is defined, but provides an alternate `static inline` no‑op stub (`{}`) when it is **not** defined — so callers can invoke it unconditionally; only the real body is SAE-gated.

## Functions that always exist but have only PART of their body conditional ("partial")

1. `hostapd_ctrl_iface_sta_mib` — `src/ap/ctrl_iface_ap.c:85` (block 148–155: appends `sae_group=` to STA MIB output)
2. `handle_auth` — `src/ap/ieee802_11.c:879` — **two** separate SAE fragments in this one function:
   - 954–957: adds `WLAN_AUTH_SAE` to the accepted-auth-algorithm check
   - 1180–1200: `case WLAN_AUTH_SAE:` branch dispatching to `handle_auth_sae`
3. `check_assoc_ies` — `src/ap/ieee802_11.c:1334` (block 1538–1560: validates PMKSA/AKM consistency for SAE-based association)
4. `ap_free_sta` — `src/ap/sta_info.c:155` (block 313–316: frees `sta->sae`)
5. `hostapd_wpa_auth_get_psk` — `src/ap/wpa_auth_glue.c:223` (block 231–237: returns `sta->sae->pmk` for SAE-authenticated STAs)
6. `wpa_write_rsn_ie` — `src/ap/wpa_auth_ie.c:91` (block 191–202: adds `SAE`/`FT-SAE` AKM suites to the RSN IE)
7. `wpa_validate_wpa_ie` — `src/ap/wpa_auth_ie.c:474` — **two** separate SAE fragments:
   - 524–529: selects the reported AKM suite for SAE/FT-SAE
   - 606–611: sets `sm->wpa_key_mgmt` for SAE/FT-SAE
8. `rsn_key_mgmt_to_bitfield` (static) — `src/common/wpa_common.c:467` (block 485–490: maps RSN SAE/FT-SAE selectors to internal bitfield values)
9. `wpa_gen_wpa_ie_rsn` (static) — `src/rsn_supp/wpa_ie.c:108` (block 173–178: emits SAE/FT-SAE AKM selector into the generated RSN IE)

## Non-function sites (excluded from the counts above, listed for completeness)

- `src/ap/hostapd.h:268-273` — struct field additions to `struct hostapd_data` (`sae_token_key`, `last_sae_token_key_update`, `dot11RSNASAERetransPeriod`) — data members, not a function.
- `src/ap/sta_info.h:162-164` — struct field `sae` in `struct sta_info` — data member, not a function.
- `src/common/Makefile:14` — `CFLAGS += -DCONFIG_SAE`, the build-system flag that actually turns the feature on for this compile config — not a source-level conditional.

## Totals

- **10 files** contain `CONFIG_SAE` conditionals in `src/`.
- **15 `#ifdef CONFIG_SAE` blocks** total.
- **23 functions** affected: **14 "whole"** (all in `ieee802_11.c`) + **9 "partial"** (across `ctrl_iface_ap.c`, `ieee802_11.c`, `sta_info.c`, `wpa_auth_glue.c`, `wpa_auth_ie.c`, `wpa_common.c`, `rsn_supp/wpa_ie.c`).
- Plus 1 header-declaration split (`sae_clear_retransmit_timer`'s real/stub pair) and 2 struct-field-only sites that don't count as functions.
